jokes.text: I DON'T LIKE YOU. BUT I RESPECT THE EFFORT.
jokes.setup: Process server tricks Ron with fake steak offer
jokes.punchline: Ron's begrudging respect for being outsmarted
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - perfectly economical line that captures his character's respect for competence even when it works against him.

jokes.text: $13,000 FOR ORTHODONTIC SURGERY, $6,000 FOR ENAMEL REPAIR, AND $46 MILLION FOR PSYCHOLOGICAL DAMAGES.
jokes.setup: Ron reads off the damage claims from being punched
jokes.punchline: The absurd leap from reasonable medical costs to $46 million in psychological damages
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure - the psychological damages amount is so outrageous it becomes absurd comedy.

jokes.text: YEAH, THAT SEEMS ABOUT ACCURATE.
jokes.setup: Ron lists the absurd $46 million lawsuit damages
jokes.punchline: Andy casually agrees that psychological damages from one punch could be worth $46 million
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - his oblivious acceptance of the ridiculous makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: WE SHOULD SUE JAMM'S PARENTS FOR SPAWNING A HUMAN TURDBURGER.
jokes.setup: Everyone agrees Jamm is awful
jokes.punchline: Tom's creative insult 'human turdburger' and the absurd idea of suing his parents for creating him
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom - unnecessarily elaborate insult. 'Turdburger' is memorable if juvenile.

jokes.text: I'LL REPRESENT MYSELF, AS I DO IN ALL LEGAL MATTERS AND LIVESTOCK AUCTIONS.
jokes.setup: Andy offers to be Ron's lawyer
jokes.punchline: Ron equating legal matters with livestock auctions as if they're the same category of self-representation
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - perfectly captures Ron's worldview where livestock auctions and legal proceedings are equivalent situations.

jokes.text: I WISH WE HAD $46 MILLION. WE COULD BUY MY PARENTS' HOUSE AND EVICT THEM.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the large lawsuit amount
jokes.punchline: April's casually dark fantasy of using money specifically to evict her own parents
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - taking a hypothetical windfall and immediately imagining the most antisocial use for it.

jokes.text: AGHH! [crash] - APRIL, COULD YOU CALL MAINTENANCE PLEASE?
jokes.setup: Someone commenting Ron is taking the lawsuit well
jokes.punchline: Ron immediately has a violent outburst, then calmly requests maintenance
jokes.explanation: Great timing and contrast between the violent outburst and immediate return to polite normalcy. Classic physical comedy structure.

jokes.text: I GUARANTEE THAT NONE OF THEM HAD AS CUTE A BUTT AS YOU DO.
jokes.setup: Leslie talking about Ben being the only mayor not to get a key to the city
jokes.punchline: Her pivot from serious political discussion to commenting on Ben's butt
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - her inability to separate professional admiration from personal attraction to Ben.

jokes.text: YOU'RE SO SEXY WHEN YOU TALK ABOUT PERCENTAGES.
jokes.setup: Ben mentions the 30% unemployment rate he caused
jokes.punchline: Leslie finds economic statistics sexually appealing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - her political nerd attraction to data points is both character-specific and absurd.

jokes.text: THIS LAWSUIT IS CHRONICLES OF RIDDICK-ULOUS.
jokes.setup: Tom expressing that the lawsuit is ridiculous
jokes.punchline: His unnecessarily elaborate pun combining 'ridiculous' with the movie title
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom - over-engineered pop culture wordplay. Fits character but the execution is forced.

jokes.text: THE NURSE STILL WON'T HAVE SEX WITH ME, AND SHE'S NOT EVEN QUEER.
jokes.setup: Jamm explaining why he's suing everyone
jokes.punchline: His oblivious misogyny and assumption that sexuality is the only reason a woman wouldn't want him
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy - Jamm's awful personality comes through clearly, though the humor relies on his awfulness.

jokes.text: EVERYONE CALLS IT THAT. / YEAH, BECAUSE I INVENTED IT.
jokes.setup: Jamm claims 'payback time' is what his family calls revenge
jokes.punchline: Tom points out everyone says that, Jamm claims he invented the phrase
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Jamm's delusional ego, though the setup is fairly standard.

jokes.text: I'M NOT NOT GOING TO WIN NOTHING.
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether Jamm will win the lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Jamm's accidentally contradictory double negative that actually means he will win nothing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - grammatically funny and reveals Jamm's stupidity.

jokes.text: THEY ONCE GOT HIM $60,000 'CAUSE HE GOT TOO SCARED AT A HAUNTED HOUSE.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jean-Ralphio's lawyers being competent
jokes.punchline: Their 'competence' includes getting money for being scared at a haunted house - the exact intended experience
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist premise - the idea of successfully suing for the intended experience of a haunted house is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: WHO WERE YOU COMPETING AGAINST? / MY OWN TASTE BUDS.
jokes.setup: Ann asks who Chris competed against in an organic gardening contest
jokes.punchline: Chris reveals he was competing against his own taste buds, implying the vegetables tasted terrible
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with perfect economy. Chris's optimistic reframing of failure is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: IF THIS WERE THE '80s, ALL WE'D HAVE TO DO IS, LIKE, NOT DROP AN EGG FOR A WHOLE DAY. STUPID INTERNET.
jokes.setup: Ann complaining about having to take a complex parenting compatibility test
jokes.punchline: Her longing for the simpler '80s egg-baby assignment and blaming the internet for overcomplication
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about how modern life overcomplicates simple things, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: OH, SNAP! I JUST FILLED IT OUT FOR FUN. CHRIS AND I ARE 81% COMPATIBLE.
jokes.setup: Ann and Chris got 58% compatibility on their parenting test
jokes.punchline: Donna casually reveals she and Chris would be much better parents together
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes Ann and Chris's relationship look worse by comparison. Classic Donna confidence.

jokes.text: ANN AND I GOT 84%.
jokes.setup: Everyone's taking the compatibility test
jokes.punchline: Tom casually reveals he and Ann would be even better parents than Donna and Chris
jokes.explanation: Good escalation continuing the joke, though Tom's delivery is more matter-of-fact than Donna's confident swagger.

jokes.text: WHO WERE BEN'S HIGH SCHOOL GIRLFRIENDS, AND WHAT BASE DO YOU THINK HE GOT TO?
jokes.setup: Leslie starts asking Ben's sister questions as if it's an interview
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriate personal question about Ben's sexual history with his sister present
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Leslie's complete lack of boundaries with her investigative enthusiasm is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: DON'T KNOW. WELL, I POOCHED THE BRAIN EXAM. JUST TO BE SAFE, I WOULDN'T TAKE ANYTHING I SAY TOO SERIOUSLY.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why he couldn't become a police officer
jokes.punchline: His advice not to take his testimony seriously immediately after giving it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - his oblivious undermining of his own credibility while trying to be helpful.

jokes.text: THE GUY'S, LIKE, A VEGETARIAN. LOOK AT HIM, HE'S LIKE A BIG, OLD, SOFT TEDDY BEAR. HIS NICKNAME AROUND THE OFFICE IS 'SOFTY-PANTS MCHUGGABLE.'
jokes.setup: April asked if Ron ever threatened anyone
jokes.punchline: Her describing the most carnivorous, tough man as a vegetarian teddy bear with an absurd nickname
jokes.explanation: Excellent lying testimony - April's creative fiction is both hilarious and perfectly opposite to Ron's actual character. 'Softy-pants McHuggable' is inspired.

jokes.text: ALSO, MAY I SAY FOR THE RECORD, THAT IS A DOPE POCKET SQUARE. CAN I SEE THAT BAD BOY?
jokes.setup: April is giving testimony in a serious legal proceeding
jokes.punchline: She completely breaks character to compliment and examine the lawyer's pocket square
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - April's complete disregard for the seriousness of the situation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: THERE'S ONLY ONE THING I HATE MORE THAN LYING. SKIM MILK. WHICH IS WATER THAT'S LYING ABOUT BEING MILK.
jokes.setup: Ron talking about how much he hates lying
jokes.punchline: His bizarre but logical comparison of skim milk to lying water
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Ron Swanson moment - perfectly captures his worldview with brilliant observational comedy. The skim milk metaphor is inspired and became iconic.

jokes.text: I THINK ABOUT WHERE YOU'VE BEEN AND WHERE YOU'RE GOING AND OUR FUTURE CHILDREN AND AMERICA AND JUST OUR HISTORY AS A NATION.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why Ben's speech makes her cry
jokes.punchline: Her emotional response escalating from personal to cosmic scope ending with 'our history as a nation'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie escalation - her ability to connect any emotional moment to the entire sweep of American history.

jokes.text: I THINK I'M GIVING BIRTH. IS THAT POSSIBLE? NO, RIGHT?
jokes.setup: Ben is doubled over in pain
jokes.punchline: His genuine confusion about whether male childbirth is possible
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Ben's panicked confusion adding to the humor of the kidney stone situation.

jokes.text: IT'S TWINS!
jokes.setup: Ben thinks he might be giving birth
jokes.punchline: His escalation to twins makes the impossible scenario even more absurd
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the birth joke, though the punchline is somewhat predictable after the setup.

jokes.text: DO YOU HAVE ANY IDEA HOW BEAUTIFUL YOU ARE? I'M SERIOUS. YOU'RE LIKE A FLOWER. OR, NO, LIKE A SNUG-BUG OR LIKE A SNUGGER-DOODLE OR SOMETHING LIKE THAT.
jokes.setup: Ben is high on morphine
jokes.punchline: His increasingly nonsensical attempts at romantic compliments ending with 'snugger-doodle'
jokes.explanation: Good drugged Ben comedy - his earnest attempt at romance undermined by drug-induced word invention.

jokes.text: SORRY, ICE TOWN, YOU'RE NOT GOING ANYWHERE FOR AT LEAST A DAY.
jokes.setup: Doctor addressing Ben about his medical condition
jokes.punchline: Using Ben's humiliating nickname from his political failure as a casual address
jokes.explanation: Good callback usage - the doctor's casual use of the nickname emphasizes how widely known Ben's failure is.

jokes.text: I BOUGHT 12 BABIES, AND THEN I IMMEDIATELY ABANDONED THEM AT A KROGER'S.
jokes.setup: Ann forgot the eggs they were going to use as practice babies
jokes.punchline: Her framing the forgotten eggs as abandoning 12 babies at a grocery store
jokes.explanation: Great reframing joke - treating the egg exercise seriously makes Ann's forgetfulness sound like serious child abandonment.

jokes.text: I WAS LIKE MARK Z IN THE SOCIAL 'N.'
jokes.setup: Tom bragging about his testimony performance
jokes.punchline: His unnecessarily abbreviated reference to 'The Social Network'
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom - over-complicated pop culture reference that's more about showing off than being funny.

jokes.text: I ONLY TELL THE TRUTH WHEN IT MAKES ME SOUND LIKE I'M LYING.
jokes.setup: April defending her approach to truth-telling
jokes.punchline: Her paradoxical relationship with honesty where she's only honest when it sounds fake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April line - perfectly captures her contrarian nature in a philosophical statement about truth that's both funny and character-revealing.

jokes.text: LYING IS ALL I KNOW. IT'S HOW I WAS ABLE TO SCRAPE BY ON THE STREETS OF BOMBAY AND MAKE IT ON THE INDIAN VERSION OF WHO WANTS TO BE A MILLIONAIRE AND GET REUNITED WITH THE LOVE OF MY LIFE, LATIKA.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his relationship with lying
jokes.punchline: His claiming the plot of 'Slumdog Millionaire' as his actual life story
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment - his casual appropriation of movie plots as personal history, though the reference feels somewhat forced.

jokes.text: SOMETIMES WHEN I BLOW MY NOSE, I GET A BONER. I DON'T KNOW WHY. IT JUST HAPPENS. TRUTH BOMB.
jokes.setup: Andy saying he loves telling the truth
jokes.punchline: His immediate overshare of an extremely personal and weird bodily function
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy overshare - his complete lack of filter makes this perfectly in character, though it's deliberately gross-out humor.

jokes.text: I FEEL LIKE I NEVER GAVE MILES DAVIS PROPER SHRIFT. LIKE, I JUST DIDN'T GET INTO IT.
jokes.setup: Ben is high on morphine being recorded
jokes.punchline: His stoned philosophical regret about not appreciating jazz properly
jokes.explanation: Good drugged Ben moment - the specific regret about Miles Davis feels authentic to his nerdy character even while impaired.

jokes.text: FRED THE SLED? THEY ONLY BRING HIM OUT FOR SPECIAL OCCASIONS.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions the town mascot is there
jokes.punchline: Ben's drugged excitement about a mascot named 'Fred the Sled' being brought out for special occasions
jokes.explanation: Good small-town detail - 'Fred the Sled' is a perfectly ridiculous mascot name, and Ben's drugged enthusiasm sells it.

jokes.text: WE DID A TRADE. I GAVE HIS WIFE NEW ENAMELS, HE GAVE MY WIFE NEW YABBOS.
jokes.setup: Jamm explaining his relationship with another character
jokes.punchline: His crude description of trading dental work for breast enhancement surgery
jokes.explanation: Effective Jamm moment showing his crudeness, though the humor relies heavily on shock value and his awful personality.

jokes.text: HE LOOKS LIKE HE COULD USE A SWIFT PUNCH IN THE FACE THOUGH.
jokes.setup: Ron seeing Jamm for the first time and being asked if he knows him
jokes.punchline: His immediate assessment that Jamm needs to be punched, which is exactly what he'll later do
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the punch - Ron's instant assessment proves completely accurate and foreshadows the incident.

jokes.text: The banner reading 'ICE CLOWN' instead of 'ICE TOWN'
jokes.setup: The town is supposedly honoring Ben with a ceremony
jokes.punchline: The banner reveals this is actually a setup to mock him with 'Ice Clown' instead of his actual failure 'Ice Town'
jokes.explanation: Effective visual reveal - the banner modification perfectly captures the town's continued mockery of Ben.

jokes.text: I'M JUST REALIZING NOW THAT'S NOT A TYPO.
jokes.setup: Leslie sees the Ice Clown banner
jokes.punchline: Her slow realization that the cruelty is intentional, not accidental
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - Leslie's dawning horror as she realizes the setup adds to the cringe.

jokes.text: The ice key melting in Leslie's hands
jokes.setup: Leslie is supposed to accept the key to the city on Ben's behalf
jokes.punchline: The key is made of ice and melts, symbolically destroying the honor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor - the ice key perfectly symbolizes how the town's 'honor' melts away, tying directly to the Ice Town theme.

jokes.text: UH-OH, THE KEY WAS MADE OF ICE. AND MUCH LIKE THE HOPES AND DREAMS OF THE PEOPLE OF PARTRIDGE, IT MELTED AWAY.
jokes.setup: The ice key melts
jokes.punchline: The mayor's cruel metaphor comparing the melting key to the town's destroyed hopes and dreams
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - the mayor's elaboration on the visual metaphor makes the cruelty explicit and more cutting.

jokes.text: HE HAS A CALCIFIED ROCK LODGED IN HIS PENIS!
jokes.setup: Leslie defending why Ben isn't at the ceremony
jokes.punchline: Her medically inaccurate and overly graphic description of kidney stones
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment - her passionate defense leads to medical oversharing and anatomical confusion.

jokes.text: SCREW YOU, PARTRIDGE.
jokes.setup: Leslie's increasingly heated defense of Ben
jokes.punchline: Direct insult to entire town while representing Pawnee government
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her loyalty overrides diplomacy completely. The directness and economy make it satisfying.

jokes.text: WELL, BEN WYATT'S BETTER THAN LEE JANZEN, AND HE'S BETTER THAN JUDY GARLAND.
jokes.setup: The crowd mentions their other famous residents
jokes.punchline: Leslie's passionate claim that Ben is better than Judy Garland, one of entertainment history's icons
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie escalation - her love for Ben leads her to make an absurd comparison with a legendary performer.

jokes.text: TRUTHFULLY, I BARELY REGISTERED HIS ATTACK. HE'S INCREDIBLY FRAIL, AND HIS ARMS ARE WEAK.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining the fight in his testimony
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact description of Jamm as physically pathetic
jokes.explanation: Good Ron testimony - his completely honest assessment delivered with typical deadpan style.

jokes.text: YOU GOT JAMMED BY YOUR OWN TEAM. SELF-JAMM!
jokes.setup: Jamm thinking he's won the case because of the testimony
jokes.punchline: His creation of 'Self-Jamm' as a play on his catchphrase when people jam themselves
jokes.explanation: Decent Jamm moment - extends his catchphrase concept, though the wordplay is forced.

jokes.text: GIRL. / BOY. ... BUDDHIST. / WHATEVER HE WANTS. / WHATEVER HE WANTS.
jokes.setup: Fertility counselor asking rapid-fire compatibility questions
jokes.punchline: Ann and Chris giving completely opposite answers to every question, showing their incompatibility
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of incompatibility - the rapid-fire opposing answers effectively show why they don't work as a couple.

jokes.text: SIX-TIME TOUR DE FRANCE CHAMPION.
jokes.setup: Asked about their future child's occupation
jokes.punchline: Chris's impossibly ambitious expectation for their unborn child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - his extreme optimism and athletic obsession lead to ridiculous expectations for his future child.

jokes.text: ARE YOU GUYS COMPLETE STRANGERS? IS THIS ONE OF THOSE CRAIGSLIST FATHER SITUATIONS?
jokes.setup: Doctor observing Ann and Chris's complete incompatibility
jokes.punchline: Assuming they must be strangers doing unconventional parenting arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good external perspective on their obvious incompatibility. The Craigslist reference feels contemporary and appropriate.

jokes.text: I'M ACTUALLY NOT SURE HOW MUCH MONEY I HAVE. BUT I DO KNOW HOW MANY POUNDS OF MONEY I HAVE.
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether Ron can afford to pay a large settlement
jokes.punchline: His measuring wealth by weight rather than monetary value, implying it's all in gold/precious metals
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron moment - perfectly captures his distrust of paper currency and government. The concept of measuring wealth by weight is brilliantly absurd yet logical for his character.

jokes.text: I'M FEEDING YOUR EAGLE. HE'S STARVING.
jokes.setup: Ben is still drugged and interacting with something in the mayor's office
jokes.punchline: He's trying to feed a bronze statue, not realizing it's not a real eagle
jokes.explanation: Good drugged Ben moment - his confusion about the statue being real is both sweet and absurd.

jokes.text: WILL YOU LOVE OUR CHILD AND WORK HARD TO SEE THAT HE OR SHE HAS A GOOD LIFE?
jokes.setup: Chris presents a new compatibility quiz after all their failures
jokes.punchline: The 'quiz' is just one simple, fundamental question that cuts through all the complexity
jokes.explanation: Good emotional payoff - Chris cuts through all the complications with the only question that really matters.

jokes.text: WE FOUND 11 LIES.
jokes.setup: April and Tom confronting Jamm with evidence
jokes.punchline: Specific high number of lies in his affidavit
jokes.explanation: Good specificity with the number. Shows April and Tom actually did the research work.

jokes.text: ONE TIME, OUR BOAT ALMOST TIPPED OVER, BUT THEN IT DIDN'T.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to share a meaningful childhood memory
jokes.punchline: His completely anticlimactic story where nothing actually happens
jokes.explanation: Excellent anti-joke - Ben's completely mundane 'adventure' story is perfectly economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: THAT'S NOT A GREAT STORY.
jokes.setup: Ben's anticlimactic boat story
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt assessment of the terrible story
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up - Leslie's honest reaction makes Ben's bad story even funnier.

jokes.text: HEY, LAKE'S NOT A GARBAGE CAN, PAL.
jokes.setup: Ben and Leslie throw the stolen key into the lake as a symbolic gesture
jokes.punchline: A local immediately scolds them for littering, missing the symbolism entirely
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of their romantic moment - the environmental concern trumps their symbolic gesture.

jokes.text: GET THE HELL OUT OF HERE, ICE TOWN!
jokes.setup: The local realizes who Ben is
jokes.punchline: He immediately uses the Ice Town nickname to chase them away
jokes.explanation: Good callback usage - shows that Ben's reputation follows him everywhere in this town.
